description_long: This object is a stylized, multi-barreled rotary rifle mounted on a hexagonal base, reminiscent of a Gatling gun. The main body is a robust blue housing with visible ventilation slits and a hand-crank mechanism at the rear, suggesting manual operation. The front features a cluster of cylindrical barrels, each with a pointed, pencil-like projectile, held together by two circular plates and reinforced by metallic rods. The rifle is supported by a tripod with three metallic legs, each anchored to the base with tan-colored mounts. Scattered around the base are several loose projectiles, matching those loaded in the barrels. The overall design combines metallic and plastic elements, with a glossy blue finish and tan accents, giving it a mechanical yet playful appearance.
description: A blue, multi-barreled rotary rifle with a hand-crank, mounted on a tripod base and loaded with tan, pencil-like projectiles. The design is robust and mechanical, with glossy blue and tan finishes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the clustered barrels loaded with tan projectiles and the circular mounting plates, with projectiles scattered on the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the blue housing, metallic rods, and the tripod legs, with the barrels extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the hand-crank mechanism and the back of the blue housing, with the tripod and mounts visible.
description_view_3: Angled front-side view showing both the hand-crank and the loaded barrels, as well as additional projectiles on the base.
